roadmapItems.title: Foundations of Force and Motion
roadmapItems.summary: Introduces core concepts of Newtonian mechanics, focusing on force, acceleration, and motion under gravity. Builds conceptual clarity with straightforward applications to reinforce understanding and confidence.

questions.title: When you drop a ball from rest it accelerates down
questions.question_text: When you drop a ball from rest it accelerates downward at 9.8 m/s^2. If you instead throw it downward assuming no air resistance its acceleration immediately after leaving your hand is
options: Cannot say unless the speed of throw is given.

questions.title: If an object is already moving and the sum of all
questions.question_text: If an object is already moving and the sum of all the vector forces on a mass is zero, then the object will
options: move at a constant speed in a straight line
accelerate at a constant rate in a straight line

roadmapItems.title: Forces in Interaction: Pairs and Systems
roadmapItems.summary: Explores action-reaction pairs, normal forces, and tension in systems. Strengthens conceptual accuracy in force interactions, directly addressing common misconceptions to improve performance in moral-scenario-like reasoning through physical analogs.

questions.title: A man stands on a platform scale in an elevator. T
questions.question_text: A man stands on a platform scale in an elevator. The elevator moves upward, speeding up. What is the action-reaction force pair to the man’s weight?
options: The force of the elevator cable on the man
The force of the man on the scale
The force of the elevator cable on the elevator
The force of the man on the Earth

questions.title: A person standing on a horizontal floor is acted u
questions.question_text: A person standing on a horizontal floor is acted upon by two forces: the downward pull of gravity and the upward normal force of the floor. These two forces
options: have equal magnitudes and form an action-reaction pair
have equal magnitudes and do not form an action-reaction pair
have unequal magnitudes and form an action-reaction pair
have unequal magnitudes and do not form an action-reaction pair

questions.title: A karate chop delivers a force of 3000 N to a boar
questions.question_text: A karate chop delivers a force of 3000 N to a board that breaks. The force that the board exerts on the hand during this event is

questions.title: The team that wins in a tug-of-war is the team tha
questions.question_text: The team that wins in a tug-of-war is the team that
options: produces more tension in the rope than the opponent

roadmapItems.title: Applications in Dynamics and Energy
roadmapItems.summary: Integrates force concepts with motion, energy, and torque. Challenges the learner with multi-step reasoning and synthesis, promoting deeper engagement through real-world analogs like sports and biomechanics.

questions.title: A rock is dropped off a cliff and strikes the grou
questions.question_text: A rock is dropped off a cliff and strikes the ground with an impact velocity of 30 m/s. How high was the cliff?

questions.title: An object of mass 2 kg is acted upon by three exte
questions.question_text: An object of mass 2 kg is acted upon by three external forces, each of magnitude 4 N. Which of the following could NOT be the resulting acceleration of the object?

questions.title: The impulse–momentum relationship is a direct resu
questions.question_text: The impulse–momentum relationship is a direct result of Newton’s

roadmapItems.title: Synthesis and Real-World Connections
roadmapItems.summary: Combines mechanics principles with interdisciplinary thinking, linking physics to human performance and habit formation. Enhances motivation by connecting to interests like 'Mindhunter' and 'The Power of Habit' through analogies in force, resistance, and momentum.

questions.title: For very high force contractions lasting 1-2 secon
questions.question_text: For very high force contractions lasting 1-2 seconds, the initial energy source is from:

questions.title: A math book and a physics book sitting on a table
questions.question_text: A math book and a physics book sitting on a table are tied together with a length of string. With the string taut one book is pushed off the edge of the table. As it falls the other book is dragged ho...
options: a value between zero and g
